data.title: CoreWeave (CRWV) Stock Drops Amid Broad Market Sell-Off
data.description:
CoreWeave Inc (NASDAQ:CRWV) shares are down on Tuesday following a recent strong rebuttal from the company’s CEO regarding its financing strategy. The stock’s decline comes as the broader market is experiencing a mixed session, with the S&P 500 down 1.5%.

Stocks across sectors are trading lower Tuesday amid overall market weakness after President Donald Trump posted on social media that, starting February 1st, Denmark, Norway, Sweden, France, Germany, the UK, the Netherlands and Finland will be charged a 10% tariff on all goods sent to the US.

President Trump has recently escalated rhetoric about making Greenland a US territory.

CoreWeave CEO Explains Financing Strategy

In a recent podcast, CEO Michael Intrator dismissed allegations of circular financing with Nvidia, labeling such claims as "ridiculous" and mathematically unsound. He emphasized that Nvidia's $300 million investment is minimal compared to CoreWeave's total capital of over $25 billion, which supports a valuation of $42 billion.

Intrator further explained that the company's use of debt is managed through special purpose vehicles, ensuring that revenue from contracts with major clients like Microsoft and Meta Platforms is ring-fenced to cover operating expenses and lenders first. This structure is designed to mitigate risk and is comparable to traditional infrastructure financing methods.

The broader market is currently experiencing declines, with the Nasdaq down 1.6% and the S&P 500 down 1.47%. CoreWeave’s drop aligns with the overall trend, indicating that the stock is caught in a broader sell-off rather than moving against positive market conditions.

Is CoreWeave Poised For A Breakout?

CoreWeave is currently trading 27.1% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) but is 1.3% below its 100-day SMA, indicating some short-term strength while showing longer-term weakness. Shares have increased by 142.29% over the past 12 months and are currently positioned closer to their 52-week highs than lows.

The RSI is at 62.18, which is considered neutral territory, suggesting that the stock is neither overbought nor oversold. Meanwhile, MACD is above its signal line, indicating bullish momentum.

The combination of neutral RSI and bullish MACD suggests mixed momentum.

  • Key Resistance: $105.50
  • Key Support: $85.00

Analysts Are Divided On CRWV Stock

Investors are looking ahead to the next earnings report on February 14.

  • EPS Estimate: Loss of 65 cents
  • Revenue Estimate: $1.54 billion

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $125.52. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Barclays: Equal-Weight (Lowered Target to $90.00) (Jan. 12)
  • Wells Fargo: Overweight (Lowered Target to $125.00) (Jan. 8)
  • Truist Securities: Initiated with Hold (Target $84.00) (Jan. 6)

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for CoreWeave, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Value: Risk (Score: 6.35/100) — Trading at a steep premium relative to peers.

The Verdict: CoreWeave’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a classic ‘High-Flyer’ setup. While the Momentum indicates some strength, the low Value score warns that the stock is priced for perfection—investors should exercise caution.

Top ETF Exposure

  • Tradr 2X Long CRWV Daily ETF (NASDAQ:CWVX): 118.53% Weight
  • iShares US Digital Infrastructure and Real Estate ETF (NYSE:IDGT): 4.65% Weight

Significance: Because CRWV carries such a heavy weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows for these ETFs will likely force automatic buying or selling of the stock.

CRWV Price Action

CRWV Price Action: CoreWeave shares were down 4.67% at $96.45 at the time of publication on Tuesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

data.title: Novavax Shares Rise After Licensing Deal With Pfizer
data.description:
Novavax, Inc. (NASDAQ:NVAX) shares are trading higher Tuesday after the company announced it entered into a license agreement with Pfizer, Inc. (NYSE:PFE) for use of its Matrix-M adjuvant.

Licensing Agreement Details

Under the non-exclusive agreement, Pfizer will be able to use Novavax's Matrix-M adjuvant in its products across up to two disease areas, the companies said. Novavax will receive a $30 million upfront payment, plus the potential for up to $500 million in development and sales milestones, and is eligible for tiered high mid-single digit royalties on products that include Matrix-M. Pfizer will be responsible for development and commercialization, while Novavax will supply the adjuvant.

The companies did not disclose specific products or timelines tied to the licensed use of the adjuvant. The deal marks another collaboration for Novavax's Matrix-M technology and adds to its efforts to monetize its vaccine platform.

Technical Analysis

The stock is trading 9.3% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 4.6% below its 100-day SMA, indicating some short-term weakness while the longer-term trend remains intact. Over the past 12 months, shares have decreased by 8.85% and are currently positioned closer to their 52-week lows than highs, reflecting a challenging year for the stock.

The RSI is at a neutral level, suggesting that the stock is neither overbought nor oversold, while the MACD is below its signal line, indicating some bearish pressure. The combination of neutral RSI and bearish MACD suggests mixed momentum, leaving traders cautious.

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Novavax, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Momentum: Neutral (Score: 37.48/100) — The stock is not currently outperforming the broader market.
  • Value: Risk (Score: 46.34/100) — Trading at a steep discount relative to peers.

The Verdict: Novavax’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a stock that is struggling to gain traction in a bearish market. While the Value score suggests it may be undervalued, the low Momentum score indicates that traders should be cautious in the current environment.

NVAX Price Action: At the time of writing, Novavax shares are trading 2.25% higher at $8.19 according to data from Benzinga Pro.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: Two Countries Just Banned Polymarket In 48 Hours—What Is Going On?
data.description:
Polymarket has been issued orders to cease operations in Portugal and Hungary amid attracting millions in bets on elections in both countries.

Portugal Bans Political Betting After Election Surge

Portugal’s gambling regulator SRIJ (Serviço de Regulação e Inspeção de Jogos) ordered Polymarket to cease operations after bets on the January 18 presidential election exceeded 103 million euros ($120 million) in trading volume.

The crackdown stems from Portugal’s 2015 online gambling law, which explicitly prohibits betting on political events. 

The platform operates without a license in the country, where only sports betting, casino games, and horse racing are legal.

“The website is not authorized to offer betting in Portugal, as national law prohibits betting operations regarding political events, be they domestic or international,” the regulator said.

While Polymarket remains accessible for now, regulators may order internet service providers to block the site entirely if the platform doesn’t comply. 

Meanwhile, competitors including Kalshi, Myriad, and Limitless still appear accessible in the country.

Hungary Becomes Second Ban This Week

Just one day before Portugal’s announcement, Hungary’s regulatory authority SZTEH banned Polymarket on January 19, citing suspicions of organizing illegal gambling.

The ban affects users accessing the site from Hungarian IP addresses, though VPN users can still connect. 

The measure remains temporary until a final decision in the case, but the timing signals coordinated regulatory pressure across Europe.

Behind Hungary’s move: Polymarket hosted $2.5 million worth of bets on the country’s next prime minister, with markets showing Péter Magyar at 56% probability versus Viktor Orbán at 43% in December. 

That level of political betting activity drew immediate regulatory attention.

Over 30 Countries Now Restrict Polymarket

Polymarket, founded in 2020, now faces restrictions in over 30 countries including Singapore, Russia, Belgium, Italy, and Ukraine—and the list is accelerating.

The enforcement varies by jurisdiction. Belgium blacklisted the site entirely, while France implemented a middle-ground approach allowing “view-only” mode where local users can see markets but can’t place bets.

The platform gained massive attention during the 2024 U.S. presidential election, where users bet hundreds of millions on the outcome. That visibility triggered increased regulatory scrutiny globally.

The Portugal and Hungary bans highlight how quickly regulators are moving against prediction markets when political betting volumes spike.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Why AST SpaceMobile (ASTS) Stock Is Flat Despite Recent Defense Win
data.description:
AST SpaceMobile Inc (NASDAQ:ASTS) shares are trading flat on Tuesday, pulling back following recent strength, as the company has secured a prime position on the U.S. Missile Defense Agency’s SHIELD program. The broader market is experiencing a downturn, with the Nasdaq-100 down 1.53% and the S&P 500 down 1.46%.

Stocks across sectors are trading lower Tuesday amid overall market weakness after President Donald Trump posted on social media that, starting February 1st, Denmark, Norway, Sweden, France, Germany, the UK, the Netherlands and Finland will be charged a 10% tariff on all goods sent to the US.

President Trump has recently escalated rhetoric about making Greenland a US territory.

  • AST SpaceMobile stock is showing upward bias recently. Where are ASTS shares going?

AST SpaceMobile’s Defense Positioning

AST SpaceMobile has been awarded a prime position on the U.S. Missile Defense Agency’s Scalable Homeland Innovative Enterprise Layered Defense (SHIELD) program, which could lead to future task orders.

This contract allows the company to compete for work related to research, development, engineering and operational support tied to U.S. missile defense modernization.

The SHIELD program is part of a broader initiative known as “Golden Dome,” which aims to enhance defense against various threats. Chief Commercial Officer Chris Ivory emphasized that being selected for this program validates AST SpaceMobile’s unique technology and growing capabilities within the defense sector.

Short-Term Weakness Raises Caution Flags

The stock is currently trading 3.5% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 5.7% below its 100-day SMA, suggesting some short-term weakness. Shares have increased approximately 390.48% over the past 12 months and are currently positioned closer to their 52-week highs than lows, reflecting a strong longer-term trend.

The RSI is not available for analysis, indicating neutral momentum, while MACD data is also not provided. The lack of these indicators means traders should be cautious as they assess the stock’s momentum.

  • Key Resistance: $120.00
  • Key Support: $110.00

Revenue Growth Amidst Projected Losses

Investors are looking ahead to the next earnings report on March 2.

  • EPS Estimate: Loss of 19 cents (Down from Loss of 18 cents YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: $39.03 million (Up from $1.92 million YoY)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Hold Rating with an average price target of $61.08. Recent analyst moves include:

The Verdict: AST SpaceMobile’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a classic ‘High-Flyer’ setup. While the Momentum score confirms the strong trend, investors should remain cautious given the mixed technical indicators.

data.title: Caviar Seller Top Wealth Group Strikes $125 Million Deal To Expand Into Fine Wine
data.description:
Top Wealth Group Holding Limited (NASDAQ:TWG) stock rose Tuesday following the company’s announcement of a material definitive agreement to acquire Airentity International Limited, a wine trading group.

This strategic move aims to diversify the company’s beverage offerings, contributing to the stock’s significant 111.49% premarket gain amid broader market declines.

TWG’s $125M Deal Boosts Luxury Portfolio

The acquisition, valued at approximately $125 million, will enhance Top Wealth’s profitability and asset size by integrating Airentity’s wine authentication and trading systems. The deal includes the issuance of nearly 15 million Class A Ordinary Shares and 3 million Class B Ordinary Shares to the vendors, which was approved by the company’s independent directors to ensure fairness and alignment with shareholder interests.

“This acquisition not only broadens our product mix but also deepens our presence in the luxury segment where caviar and fine wine naturally complement each other. We are confident in our unified vision and future growth prospects.”, commented Kim Kwan Kings, Wong.

The acquisition aligns with Top Wealth’s existing premium caviar business, as both sectors cater to similar customer groups. The company believes this strategic expansion will deepen its presence in the luxury segment, where caviar and fine wine naturally complement each other.

TWG Faces 85.41% Yearly Decline; Near 52-Week Low

Currently, the stock is trading at $7.36, which is significantly down 85.41% over the past 12 months. The stock is closer to its 52-week lows than to its highs, indicating a challenging longer-term trend.

The RSI is not available at the moment, suggesting neutral momentum, while the MACD is also unavailable, leaving traders without clear signals. The absence of these indicators means that the stock’s momentum remains uncertain.

  • Key Resistance: $8.50

Benzinga Ranks TWG High on Value, Low on Momentum

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Top Wealth Group Holding Limited Class A Ordinary Shares, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Value: Strong (Score: 99.83/100) — The stock is considered undervalued relative to its peers.
  • Momentum: Bearish (Score: 0.73/100) — The stock is underperforming the broader market.

The Verdict: Top Wealth Group Holding Limited Class A Ordinary Shares’ Benzinga Edge signal reveals a classic divergence in performance. While the Value score suggests potential upside, the low Momentum score indicates significant challenges ahead, suggesting investors should approach with caution.

TWG Price Action: Top Wealth Group shares were up 111.49% at $7.36 during premarket trading on Tuesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.
